creator: chessg@gmail.com
text:
When I connect the Zoom H4 digital recorder, which should mount as a
standard USB mass storage device (it does in Linux), I get hal and dbus
crashes and my DE (GNOME or Xfce) hang.  I have another USB mass storage
device (Cowon iAudio 5) which works fine.

My /etc/rc.conf has, among other things:

dbus_enable="YES"
polkitd_enable="YES"
hald_enable="YES" 
devfs_system_ruleset="localrules"

My /etc/devfs.rules has:
[localrules=1]
add path 'da*' mode 0660 group operator

dmesg output:

How-To-Repeat: Connect Zoom H4 via USB cable.
